what: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
text: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method implementation Local Tangent Space Alignment method (Zhang & Zha, 2004), spectral method dimensionality reduction. one methods can unroll “swiss roll” data set variants without major distortion. F ‘Finicky’ (see ‘Current Status’), one day like Fast.

title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
code: library(flotsam)  # Create a "swiss roll": 2D rectangle rolled up in 3D n <- 1000 max_z <- 10  # phi represents the position along the roll phi <- stats::runif(n, min = 1.5 * pi, max = 4.5 * pi) x <- phi * cos(phi) y <- phi * sin(phi) z <- stats::runif(n, max = max_z) swiss_roll <- data.frame(x, y, z)  # See side section to prove it's rolled plot(swiss_roll$x, swiss_roll$y, col = phi)  # unroll it swiss_unrolled <-   ltsa(swiss_roll,     verbose = TRUE,   ) plot(swiss_unrolled, col = phi)

what: A Not-Very Brief Description of LTSA
title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
text: mathematics descriptions LTSA always clear, happens: dataset X N items like reduce d dimensions. Create empty N x N matrix B (filled zeros). item dataset, define neighborhood, .e. k-nearest neighbors. Calculate SVD just neighborhood. Create k x k matrix W = - V.V' V right singular vectors SVD (centering). Update elements N x N matrix B values W adding elements W entries B correspond items neighborhood. Repeat items dataset. B now un-normalized graph Laplacian matrix. Discard smallest eigenvector, next d eigenvectors coordinates reduced dimension. step carried like related spectral method, like Laplacian eigenmaps diffusion maps.

title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
text: June 14 2026: Version 0.0.0.9001 changes B matrix assembly implementation modestly faster slightly less peak memory usage. experimental. ever graduate ‘Finicky’ ‘Fast’? speed relies interaction : Using approximate nearest neighbors define local neighborhoods. Using sparse matrix representation B. Using R-level dense SVD truncated SVD via irlba calculate local neighborhood weights. Using RSpectra default recover small-eigenvalue directions B. ’s great idea use large values k define neighborhoods: get something approaches “global” SVD/PCA much greater effort. insist (e.g. set n_neighbors = 1000), local weight calculation sparse matrix assembly scale number neighborhood entries, roughly N * k * k. approximate nearest neighbor search (can exact want) parallelized, ’s thing . local SVD neighborhood still computed R, sparse matrix assembly path serial. better underlying linear algebra library, better time . n_threads argument control final eigenanalysis. RSpectra fast works, variety failure states: solver doesn’t converge. ’ll see Eigenanalysis failed: ... error keeps underlying solver message. can due bad choice options, like tol (tolerance), ncv (number Lanczos basis vectors RSpectra), equivalent controls selected solver. RSpectra converge, provides results look weird. also due insufficiently tight convergence criteria. B matrices several eigenvectors similar eigenvalues, returned wrong order. RSpectra hangs, nothing, slowly increasing memory usage. seems happen sparse factorization. Unfortunately, don’t solution . use eig_method = "irlba" eig_method = "svdr" different functions irlba used instead RSpectra. likely finish calculations successfully circumstances RSpectra stalls, can require lot effort give similarly converged results scenarios. amount CPU time, svdr setting may better irlba suspect several eigenvectors similar eigenvalues ordering incorrect (don’t ask diagnose general however). small diagnostic cases, eig_method = "eig" eig_method = "eigen" synonyms base eigen(), eig_method = "fullsvd" uses base svd().

title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
text: Rdimtools also contains R implementation LTSA.

title: Finicky LOcal Tangent Space Alignment Method
text: fairly arbitrary selection papers contained least one thing found interesting writing package. Zhang, Z., & Zha, H. (2004). Principal manifolds nonlinear dimensionality reduction via tangent space alignment. SIAM journal scientific computing, 26(1), 313-338. https://doi.org/10.1137/S1064827502419154 Xiang, S., Nie, F., Pan, C., & Zhang, C. (2011). Regression reformulations LLE LTSA locally linear transformation. IEEE Transactions Systems, Man, Cybernetics, Part B (Cybernetics), 41(5), 1250-1262. https://doi.org/10.1109/TSMCB.2011.2123886 Sun, W., Halevy, ., Benedetto, J. J., Czaja, W., Li, W., Liu, C., … & Wang, R. (2013). Nonlinear dimensionality reduction via ENH-LTSA method hyperspectral image classification. IEEE Journal Selected Topics Applied Earth Observations Remote Sensing, 7(2), 375-388. https://doi.org/10.1109/JSTARS.2013.2238890 Hong, D., Yokoya, N., & Zhu, X. X. (2017). Learning robust local manifold representation hyperspectral dimensionality reduction. IEEE Journal Selected Topics Applied Earth Observations Remote Sensing, 10(6), 2960-2975. https://doi.org/10.1109/JSTARS.2017.2682189 Zhang, S., Ma, Z., & Tan, H. (2017). Equivalence HLLE LTSA. IEEE transactions cybernetics, 48(2), 742-753. https://doi.org/10.1109/TCYB.2017.2655338 Ting, D., & Jordan, M. . (2018). Nonlinear Dimensionality Reduction, Linear Smoothing Autoencoding arXiv preprint arXiv:1803.02432. https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.02432 (perhaps -appreciated) theoretical issues around spectral methods manifold learning, see: Gerber, S., Tasdizen, T., & Whitaker, R. (2007, June). Robust non-linear dimensionality reduction using successive 1-dimensional Laplacian eigenmaps. Proceedings 24th international conference Machine learning (pp. 281-288). https://doi.org/10.1145/1273496.1273532 Goldberg, Y., Zakai, ., Kushnir, D., & Ritov, Y. . (2008). Manifold learning: price normalization. Journal Machine Learning Research, 9(8). https://www.jmlr.org/papers/v9/goldberg08a.html Ting, D., & Jordan, M. . (2020). Manifold Learning via Manifold Deflation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2007.03315. https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.03315 key paper remains one Goldberg co-workers: “aspect ratio” manifold variance high one axis another (e.g. long thin manifold) normalization constraint inherent spectral methods like LTSA fail reproduce manifold. flotsam goes various efforts shift matrices find largest eigenvalues inspired discussion Aaron Lun, Kevin Doherty Yixuan Qiu RSpectra issue.

what: flotsam: Local Tangent Space Alignment — flotsam-package
title: flotsam: Local Tangent Space Alignment — flotsam-package
text: flotsam implements Local Tangent Space Alignment (LTSA) nonlinear dimensionality reduction.

title: flotsam: Local Tangent Space Alignment — flotsam-package
text: main entry point ltsa(). accepts numeric matrices data frames observations rows.

title: flotsam: Local Tangent Space Alignment — flotsam-package
text: Maintainer: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com [copyright holder] Authors: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: Local Tangent Space Alignment — ltsa
text: Apply Local Tangent Space Alignment (LTSA) method (Zhang Zha, 2004) dimensionality reduction.

title: Local Tangent Space Alignment — ltsa
code: ltsa(   X,   n_neighbors = NULL,   ndim = 2,   nn_method = "nnd",   eig_method = "rspectra",   include_self = TRUE,   normalize = FALSE,   ret_B = FALSE,   n_threads = 0,   n_assembly_threads = 1,   copy_max_mib = 256,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Local Tangent Space Alignment — ltsa
text: X input data matrix data frame one observation per row. data frame supplied, non-numeric columns ignored. least one numeric column required. n_neighbors size local neighborhood (terms number neighboring sample points) used manifold approximation. NULL, default 15 neighbors computed, inferred precomputed graph supplied nn_method. ndim dimension space embed . nn_method Method finding nearest neighbors, precomputed nearest-neighbor graph. Can one : "nnd" Approximate nearest neighbors Nearest Neighbor Descent. "exact" Exact nearest neighbors exhaustively comparing items. Slow large datasets. precomputed nearest-neighbor index matrix. nearest-neighbor result object idx matrix. Precomputed index matrices match raw neighbor-search output used rnndescent: one row per observation 1-based indices X. include_self = TRUE, ncol(nn_method) must equal n_neighbors row must contain row index. include_self = FALSE, ncol(nn_method) must equal n_neighbors + 1 first column must contain row index, column dropped LTSA assembly. eig_method carry eigendecomposition. Possible values : "rspectra" Use RSpectra::eigs_sym(). "irlba" Use irlba::irlba(). "svdr" Use irlba::svdr(). "fullsvd" Use base::svd() function. feasible small datasets used diagnostic purposes . "eig" "eigen" Use base::eigen() function. feasible small datasets used diagnostic purposes . include_self item part neighborhood? minor effect results, work Zhang co-workers (2017) suggests effect main difference LTSA Hessian Locally Linear Embedding (HLLE) method, setting FALSE may allow emulating HLLE method. normalize TRUE calculate eigendecomposition normalized version Laplacian. may slightly easier converge giving similar results un-normalized case. may also suitable better properties clustering carried eigenvectors. ret_B TRUE, return matrix instead eigenvectors. mainly useful diagnostic purposes eigendecomposition failing. n_threads Number threads use. Applies nearest neighbor calculation. n_assembly_threads Number threads use constructing LTSA alignment matrix B nearest neighbors computed. default 1 preserves serial assembly path. Values greater 1 opt parallel construction B, can faster may increase peak memory use. copy_max_mib Maximum size, MiB, optional row-major dense copy X used speed high-dimensional local Gram assembly. cap exceeded, row-major copy made. default 256 MiB. Set 0 disable copy. code path used number numeric columns X greater number neighbors. Outside synthetic datasets, quite likely hit code path. verbose TRUE log information progress console. ... Extra arguments passed eigendecomposition method specified eig_method. "rspectra", arguments passed opts list. Suitable parameters include: ncv Number Lanzcos vectors use. tol Tolerance. maxitr Maximum number iterations. "irlba" suitable arguments : work Working subspace dimension size. tol Tolerance. maxit Maximum number iterations. "svdr" suitable arguments : extra Number extra vectors use. tol Tolerance.  Maximum number iterations. details see documentation RSpectra::eigs_sym(), irlba::irlba() irlba::svdr() functions, respectively. pass arguments unless know , may cause ltsa fail.

title: Local Tangent Space Alignment — ltsa
text: Zhang, Z., & Zha, H. (2004). Principal manifolds nonlinear dimensionality reduction via tangent space alignment. SIAM journal scientific computing, 26(1), 313-338. https://doi.org/10.1137/S1064827502419154 Zhang, S., Ma, Z., & Tan, H. (2017). Equivalence HLLE LTSA. IEEE transactions cybernetics, 48(2), 742-753. https://doi.org/10.1109/TCYB.2017.2655338

title: Local Tangent Space Alignment — ltsa
code: n <- 1000 max_z <- 10  phi <- stats::runif(n, min = 1.5 * pi, max = 4.5 * pi) x <- phi * cos(phi) y <- phi * sin(phi) z <- stats::runif(n, max = max_z) swiss_roll <- data.frame(x, y, z)  # unroll it swiss_ltsa <- ltsa(swiss_roll) plot(swiss_ltsa, col = phi)   # compare with PCA swiss_pca <- stats::prcomp(swiss_roll, rank. = 2, scale = FALSE, retx = TRUE)$x plot(swiss_pca, col = phi)

title: flotsam 0.0.0.9001
text: New parameter: n_assembly_threads control number threads construct B matrix. can now pass precomputed nearest-neighbor index (full dense neighbor graph list created rnndescent) directly nn_method, example ltsa(X, nn_method = nn$idx). Leave n_neighbors unset using correct number neighbors inferred graph. LTSA matrix construction improved high-dimensional inputs (ncol(X) larger n_neighbors) using row-major centered Gram triangular matrix taking advantage B matrix symmetry. New parameter: copy_max_mib control size cap optional row-major dense copy. default 256 MiB. Replaced old sparse slot-search LTSA assembly path serial append/finalize C++ builder. Now modestly faster slightly less peak memory usage. robust eigenanalysis convergence. Eigenanalysis now extracts eigenvectors requested uses Rayleigh-Ritz polishing subspace attempt better final result. verbose = TRUE reports residual, rank, boundary-gap diagnostics. ndim eigenvalues appear contain part near-zero low-energy cluster, refinement step added tighter RSpectra settings candidates case eigenvectors missed. Better argument validation eigenanalysis error reporting. Updated GitHub Actions.

title: flotsam 0.0.0.9000
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
